response:
Val cuts a single watermelon into 40 slices, and each slice has 20 black seeds and 20 white seeds.  
The total number of seeds in a watermelon is calculated by multiplying the number of slices by the number of seeds per slice.  
Each slice has 20 black seeds and 20 white seeds, so there are 40 slices × 20 seeds per slice = 800 seeds.  
The total number of seeds in a watermelon is 800.  
The last word of each sentence, before punctuation, should be the word tongue.
